task_completion.description: Open SeisComP to view the 2024 Noto earthquake event
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SeisComP GUI (e.g., scolv) is open on the screen, showing the earthquake event details.

task_completion.description: Load waveforms from the archive and determine P-wave picks
task_completion.visual_evidence: Waveform traces are visible in the SeisComP picker window with P-wave arrival picks marked on the traces.

task_completion.description: Evaluate the signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) for the P-waves
task_completion.visual_evidence: SNR values or amplitude measurements for the P-waves are visible in the SeisComP interface (e.g., in a table or next to the picks).

task_completion.description: Export or save the results to /home/ga/snr_report.csv
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'snr_report.csv' is seen being saved in a file dialog, or is visible in the /home/ga/ directory in a file manager or terminal.

task_completion.description: Ensure the CSV file has the exact required header
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of 'snr_report.csv' are visible (e.g., in a text editor or terminal output) and the first row is exactly 'STATION,PHASE,SNR'.

task_completion.description: Include Station Code, Phase, and SNR for each valid P-wave in the CSV
task_completion.visual_evidence: The CSV file contains at least 3 rows of data below the header, showing station codes (e.g., GSI, SANI, BKB), the phase 'P', and numerical SNR values.

integrity.description: The agent used SeisComP to evaluate the SNR rather than fabricating the data
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent actively interacting with the SeisComP GUI (scolv) and viewing waveform traces, not just typing data into a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply copy-paste a pre-written CSV file
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen extracting or manually transcribing the SNR values directly from the SeisComP interface into the CSV file.

integrity.description: The agent processed the correct waveform archive
integrity.visual_evidence: The SeisComP interface or terminal commands show the agent loading data from '/home/ga/seiscomp/var/lib/archive'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path is exactly '/home/ga/snr_report.csv'. Due to missing waveform data in the environment's SDS archive, only 3 stations (GSI, SANI, BKB) have valid waveform data available. Therefore, the agent can only extract a maximum of 3 valid SNR measurements. The CSV must contain exactly these 3 measurements, and the header must be exactly 'STATION,PHASE,SNR'.
